2603.23245.title: Neural ODE and SDE Models for Adaptation and Planning in Model-Based Reinforcement Learning
2603.23245.paper_summary: We investigate neural ordinary and stochastic differential equations (neural ODEs and SDEs) to model stochastic dynamics in fully and partially observed environments within a model-based reinforcement learning (RL) framework. Through a sequence of simulations, we show that neural SDEs more effectively capture the inherent stochasticity of transition dynamics, enabling high-performing policies with improved sample efficiency in challenging scenarios. We leverage neural ODEs and SDEs for efficient policy adaptation to changes in environment dynamics via inverse models, requiring only limited interactions with the new environment. To address partial observability, we introduce a latent SDE model that combines an ODE with a GAN-trained stochastic component in latent space. Policies derived from this model provide a strong baseline, outperforming or matching general model-based and model-free approaches across stochastic continuous-control benchmarks. This work demonstrates the applicability of action-conditional latent SDEs for RL planning in environments with stochastic transitions. Our code is available at: https://github.com/ChaoHan-UoS/NeuralRL
2603.23245.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究神經常微分方程和隨機微分方程（神經ODE和SDE），以在基於模型的強化學習（RL）框架中建模完全和部分觀察環境中的隨機動態。<lb/>通過一系列模擬，我們顯示神經SDE能更有效地捕捉轉換動態的內在隨機性，使得在挑戰性場景中能夠實現高效能的策略並提高樣本效率。<lb/>我們利用神經ODE和SDE通過逆模型來高效地適應環境動態的變化，僅需與新環境進行有限的互動。<lb/>為了解決部分可觀察性問題，我們引入了一種潛在SDE模型，該模型在潛在空間中結合了ODE和經過GAN訓練的隨機組件。<lb/>從這個模型派生的策略提供了一個強有力的基線，在隨機連續控制基準測試中超越或匹配一般的基於模型和無模型的方法。<lb/>這項工作展示了條件於行動的潛在SDE在具有隨機轉換的環境中進行RL規劃的適用性。<lb/>我們的代碼可在以下地址獲得：https://github.com/ChaoHan-UoS/NeuralRL
2603.23245.author: Chao Han et.al.
2603.23245.authors: Chao Han, Stefanos Ioannou, Luca Manneschi, T. J. Hayward, Michael Mangan, Aditya Gilra, Eleni Vasilaki
